name: María Inés Cervantes
bio: Science-trained journalist covering varietal breeding, fermentation chemistry, and the agronomy of high-altitude coffee. Ten years at AgroNotas before going freelance.

name: Javier Ruvalcaba
bio: Geographer turned origin reporter. Writes about volcanic soils, microclimates, and the slow geography of terroir. Based in Quetzaltenango, on the road most months of the year.

name: Lucía Bermúdez
bio: Trade economist by training, smallholder advocate by vocation. Covers cooperative finance, certification economics, and the price-fixing arithmetic that shapes producer livelihoods.

name: Diego Pavón
bio: Former mill operator. Writes the technical end of the chain: micro-mill builds, processing experiments, drying-bed engineering. Skeptic of marketing copy; partial to gravity-fed wet mills.

name: Camila Torres-Vega
bio: Q-grader and sensory writer. Covers cupping, scoring methodology, and the long argument over what specialty actually means. Runs a small roastery in Bogotá on the side.

name: Andrés Fonseca
bio: Historian of Latin American agriculture. Writes the long-arc pieces: how a region became what it is, who broke the trail, and what the next generation is inheriting.
